title: How Anthropic runs large-scale code migrations with Claude Code
excerpt: A step-by-step guide to running large code migrations with AI agents — including Bun's million-line Zig-to-Rust port.
content_html:
Six steps for large code migrations

The process below has been generalized to be relevant to multiple languages and scenarios. For additional details, you can read Jarred’s blog.

Prerequisites

A prerequisite before starting on your migration project is to have a strong judge in place, otherwise you won’t have an exit condition or measure of success. 

The judge must be able to evaluate both the original code and the target code on equal terms. Test suites written in the original language will often depend on internal functions that won't exist in the target code. 

To build this judge:

  • Categorize existing tests. Use Claude to identify which tests are expressible as external calls and which depend on internals that won't port. 
  • Rewrite for portability. Convert the external-facing tests into assertions that can run against both the original and the port. Use adversarial agents to verify the rewritten tests don't weaken the assertions.
  • Validate the judge. Run it against the original code to confirm it passes. Then run it against deliberately broken code to confirm it fails — a judge that doesn't catch breakage isn't a judge.

Jarred had a large test suite written in a third language (TypeScript), but that will not be the case for most projects. For his Python-to-TypeScript port, Mike created a parity harness of seven real-world scenarios and considered any behavior change a bug to be fixed. 

Before we get into each stage, this graphic may help you follow along. This mostly follows Jarred’s methodology, with reviews and gates at each stage. Mike followed a similar overall structure using similar loop workflows, but he ran the entire migration end to end, revised the rules and the workflow based on the results, and ran it again — discarding the output each time until the third run.

Step 1 — Create the rulebook, dependency map, and gap inventory

In this stage we are creating the foundations of our migration: an inventory of places where code will need to be refactored rather than just translated, a rulebook for how to translate our code, and a dependency map to order our migration implementation workstreams. 

The order matters: the rulebook must come before the gap inventory. The gap inventory is defined by what the rulebook's defaults won't cover, and the two are tested together in a joint audit.

Rulebook

The exact shape of the rulebook depends on key architectural decisions you must make at the start. Chief among them, if the new code will follow the same structure, or if it will be completely redesigned. 

If it’s the former (Jarred), the rulebook will primarily be lookup tables that translates types and idioms between languages while pointing to the gap inventory for the harder-to-translate components. If it’s the latter (Mike), it will be a design document. 

Jarred created his rulebook by chatting with Claude, forming a policy for each area of ambiguity. He also used eight subagents specifically designed to review for 8 different categories of common failure modes based on his own intuition.

Dependency map

You need to understand file dependencies to effectively break up workstreams for a parallel migration so you know which files to migrate first and which files to contain in the same batch. Some languages and codebases have explicit manifests that make this easy, but for legacy codebases and many popular languages like C/C++ and Python, these dependencies need to be discovered and mapped.

Claude Code can deploy agents to create and run a deterministic script to produce this map. The prompt in the migration kit uses a workflow to create a review-and-fix loop. Note: The starter kit is a generalized template of the process laid out in this post — it's not what these specific ports ran on.

Gap inventory and skeptic reviewers

The new language has different requirements from the old language that must be met. For Zig to Rust the difference was manual memory management (C and C++ work the same way). For example:

Zig

fn readConfig(allocator: std.mem.Allocator) ![]u8 {
    const buf = try allocator.alloc(u8, 1024);
    // ...fill buf...
    return buf; // caller must free this — but only the comment says so
}

// A caller that forgets 'defer allocator.free(buf)' still compiles — the leak only surfaces at runtime.

Rust

fn read_config() -> Vec<u8> { 
let buf = vec![0u8; 1024]; 
// ...fill buf... 
buf // ownership moves to the caller; memory is freed automatically 
} 
// Use it after it's moved? Free it twice? Neither compiles. 
// Forget to free it? There's no free call to forget — drop is automatic.

For Python to TypeScript the gap was interfaces and contracts. Python doesn’t require a contract declaring what shape of object it will accept or what it returns, but TypeScript does. For example:

Python

def register(handler):
    handler.setup()
    return handler.run({"retries": 3})

# Any object with .setup() and .run() works here. Which objects actually get passed in? Read the whole codebase to find out.

TypeScript

interface RunResult { ok: boolean } 

interface Handler 
{ setup(): void; 
run(opts: { retries: number }): Promise<RunResult>; 
} 

function register(handler: Handler): Promise<RunResult> { 
handler.setup(); 
return handler.run({ retries: 3 }); } 

// The contract must be written down before this compiles

Both Jarred and Mike created gap inventory files capturing this implicit knowledge. Jarred inventoried these gaps up front, which is what we do here, while Mike chose to translate first and then create the gap inventory by auditing afterwards. You may need to do both. 

Check out this sample Claude Code prompt to create a gap inventory file.

Step 2 — Stress-test the rules 

This step involves a mini-migration that serves as a “shakedown cruise” for the larger migration. 

In this step, Jarred used one agent to translate three files using the rulebook, one agent to translate three files “like a senior Rust engineer,” and one agent to use the diff to create new translation rules. At this stage he caught two critical issues that would have created numerous issues if fanned out across all 1,448 files.

The prompt may look something like this.

This type of stress test only works for structure-preserving migrations, where two translations of the same file are comparable line by line.  If your rulebook is a redesign — like Mike's — the equivalent test is attacking the design document directly with adversarial reviewers, then validating it with a disposable end-to-end run.

Regardless, throw out any translated files. The goal is to refine the rules, not make incremental progress.

Step 3 — Translate everything 

For the remaining steps, you run the same multi-agent loop architecture: implement, review, and fix. 

You can offload implementer work to smaller models and keep reviewers on larger ones. For example, Mike used Claude Sonnet when he fanned out 12 subagents for the main migration. 

The work queue should be mechanical. A batch script decides what’s done by checking whether the translated file exists on disk, then slices the pending files into batches for the implementer agents. Because the queue is rebuilt from disk every time, the migration is resumable by construction.

At this stage, agents can be overly cautious with how much work they do. The fix can be a blunt, emphatic prompt instruction with context that the compiler will catch mistakes in the next step.

Anything the translator can’t execute confidently gets flagged with // TODO(port): <reason> to be dealt with in step 4. From here on, the to-do lists write themselves: the compiler enumerates the errors, the smoke tests find the crashes, the suite reports the failures.

Two adversarial reviewers evaluate the work of the implementers using separate contexts and disagreement between reviewers goes to a third agent. When a reviewer keeps catching the same mistake across files, the fix isn't per-file. You add one sentence to the rulebook and regenerate the affected batch. The rulebook keeps growing through this step; the code never gets hand-patched against it.

One important design decision to note in this step is where the compiler sits. Mike ran the TypeScript compiler inside every loop, because it checks a unit in seconds. Jarred banned the compiler from the loop entirely and deferred it to the next step, because cargo takes minutes.

At this step, much of the heavy lifting has been done and the prompts start to get shorter.

Steps 4, 5, 6 — Compile, run, and match behavior

These three steps share the same loop architecture and need progressively less human judgment, so we cover them together.

Step 4, for example, may often dissolve into step 3 depending on the language and size of the migration. 

Depending on the size and difficulty of the compiler step, agents may not run this at all. Jarred executed this with an orchestrator script that invoked the compiler once across the whole workspace. “Fixer agents” then ran through the error list in parallel with adversarial review. The build runs again, rinse and repeat. 

Reviewing the error list is helpful to catch systemic issues that may require adjustments. For example, Jarred ran into thousands of Rust module errors that surfaced after fixing cyclic imports that Zig's lazy compilation tolerated. He fixed the loop by encoding logic to classify which dependence to delete, move, or restructure the boundary. 

Step 5 also has a mechanical source of truth similar to the compiler error list: crashes from the smoke test. Again, the loop fix was to group issues into categories, in this case grouping causes by root cause that are reviewed by adversarial subagents. 

Step 6 and the end of our story is comparing the programs’ behavior across the two codebases.

Our files have now been translated, compiled, and smoke tested. Now it's time to shard them and run the test suite (from the prerequisite stage) against them. Tackle failures with "fixer agents" that review the failed tests against both codebases. Adversarial reviewers check their fixes.

The next stage in this loop is a build daemon, which is the only process allowed to rebuild the binary. Fixers write patches; the daemon batches them, rebuilds once, re-runs the affected tests, and feeds the results back. This serializes the most expensive operation instead of letting multiple agents trigger it independently.

When the same failure repeats across many tests, the fix moves upstream: you amend the rule that produced the bug and regenerate only the files that rule touched.

Mike's approach matters here, because many developers won't have a built-out or ported test suite. Mike had Claude create a small script to run 7 real-world scenarios against both the new port and the original Python codebase, and diffed the results. Each failing scenario got its own fix agent, and the loop ran until all seven passed.

Then he went one step further. Claude designed its own end-to-end test suite and ran it autonomously overnight, fixing what broke and re-running four nights in a row. As a result, it caught the paper cuts no scenario list would have predicted.

The lesson is that a missing test suite doesn't block this step. If you can't inherit a referee, have Claude build one. Your original codebase is the ground truth either way.

Code migrations best practices

Every run taught us something the previous one didn't. It’s a safe bet your next migration will teach you things this guide can't. But a few practices held up across every project:

  • Don't follow this guide blindly. Each migration is different. Treat this as a starting point, and plan your specific migration with Claude before committing to it.
  • Don’t focus on individual failures. Individual failures are the loop's job. Fixer agents burn those down. Your attention belongs on the patterns. 
  • Make review adversarial and verification mechanical. Adversarial review allows for longer running tasks and is often worth the token consumption. Let scripts — a compiler, a diff, a test suite — be the referee. 
  • Don't use the largest model for everything. Token spend concentrates in your loops, so design them deliberately. Smaller models handle the high-volume implementation fan-out well; save your largest model for reviewers and for anything that writes rules other agents will follow.
  • Front-load the human hours. The rulebook and the stress test are the most time-consuming. Everything after is mostly queues burning down. 
  • Make the work queue mechanical and resumable. Done should mean "the output file exists on disk." 

Review loop results, not code

Jarred’s Bun migration is now in production, although every migration has tradeoffs. For example, about 4% of the Rust code sits inside "unsafe" blocks, mostly single-line pointer operations at C/C++ boundaries.

But the new codebase is measurably better. Every memory leak the team's tooling can detect has been fixed: one benchmark of 2,000 repeated builds dropped from 6,745 MB of memory to 609. The binary is 19% smaller on Linux and Windows. And cross-language optimization made it 2–5% faster across HTTP serving and real-world workloads like next build and tsc. 

Consider whether it’s time to re-run the math of your long deferred migration. Pick the codebase you've been tolerating and ask Claude what the migration process looks like for it.

Related

  • Migration starter kit Note: The starter kit is a generalized template of the process above — it's not what these specific ports ran on.
  • Code-modernization plugin — for legacy modernization and framework upgrades rather than language ports
  • Dynamic workflows in Claude Code

sentences.en: In this stage we are creating the foundations of our migration: an inventory of places where code will need to be refactored rather than just translated, a rulebook for how to translate our code, and a dependency map to order our migration implementation workstreams.
sentences.zh: 在此阶段，我们正在创建迁移的基础：需要重构代码而不仅仅是翻译代码的位置清单、如何翻译代码的规则手册以及用于排序迁移实施工作流的依赖关系图。

blocks.html_en: The order matters: the rulebook must come before the gap inventory. The gap inventory is defined by what the rulebook's defaults won't cover, and the two are tested together in a joint audit.

sentences.en: The order matters: the rulebook must come before the gap inventory.
sentences.zh: 顺序很重要：规则手册必须先于缺口库存。

sentences.en: The gap inventory is defined by what the rulebook's defaults won't cover, and the two are tested together in a joint audit.
sentences.zh: 缺口库存是根据规则手册的默认设置不涵盖的内容来定义的，并且两者在联合审计中一起进行测试。

blocks.html_en: Rulebook

sentences.en: Rulebook
sentences.zh: 规则手册

blocks.html_en: The exact shape of the rulebook depends on key architectural decisions you must make at the start. Chief among them, if the new code will follow the same structure, or if it will be completely redesigned.

sentences.en: The exact shape of the rulebook depends on key architectural decisions you must make at the start.
sentences.zh: 规则手册的确切形式取决于您必须在开始时做出的关键架构决策。

sentences.en: Chief among them, if the new code will follow the same structure, or if it will be completely redesigned.
sentences.zh: 其中最主要的是，新代码是否将遵循相同的结构，或者是否将完全重新设计。

blocks.html_en: If it’s the former (Jarred), the rulebook will primarily be lookup tables that translates types and idioms between languages while pointing to the gap inventory for the harder-to-translate components. If it’s the latter (Mike), it will be a design document.

sentences.en: If it’s the former (Jarred), the rulebook will primarily be lookup tables that translates types and idioms between languages while pointing to the gap inventory for the harder-to-translate components.
sentences.zh: 如果是前者（Jarred），则规则手册将主要是查找表，用于翻译语言之间的类型和习语，同时指出难以翻译的组件的差距清单。

sentences.en: If it’s the latter (Mike), it will be a design document.
sentences.zh: 如果是后者（迈克），那么它将是一个设计文档。

blocks.html_en: Jarred created his rulebook by chatting with Claude, forming a policy for each area of ambiguity. He also used eight subagents specifically designed to review for 8 different categories of common failure modes based on his own intuition.

sentences.en: Jarred created his rulebook by chatting with Claude, forming a policy for each area of ambiguity.
sentences.zh: 贾里德通过与克劳德聊天创建了他的规则手册，为每个不明确的领域制定了政策。

sentences.en: He also used eight subagents specifically designed to review for 8 different categories of common failure modes based on his own intuition.
sentences.zh: 他还使用了专门设计的 8 个子代理，根据自己的直觉检查 8 种不同类别的常见故障模式。

blocks.html_en: Dependency map

sentences.en: Dependency map
sentences.zh: 依赖关系图

blocks.html_en: You need to understand file dependencies to effectively break up workstreams for a parallel migration so you know which files to migrate first and which files to contain in the same batch. Some languages and codebases have explicit manifests that make this easy, but for legacy codebases and many popular languages like C/C++ and Python, these dependencies need to be discovered and mapped.

sentences.en: You need to understand file dependencies to effectively break up workstreams for a parallel migration so you know which files to migrate first and which files to contain in the same batch.
sentences.zh: 您需要了解文件依赖性才能有效地分解并行迁移的工作流，以便您知道首先要迁移哪些文件以及同一批次中要包含哪些文件。

sentences.en: Some languages and codebases have explicit manifests that make this easy, but for legacy codebases and many popular languages like C/C++ and Python, these dependencies need to be discovered and mapped.
sentences.zh: 某些语言和代码库具有显式清单，使这一切变得容易，但对于遗留代码库和许多流行语言（如 C/C++ 和 Python），需要发现和映射这些依赖关系。

blocks.html_en: Claude Code can deploy agents to create and run a deterministic script to produce this map. The prompt in the migration kit uses a workflow to create a review-and-fix loop. Note: The starter kit is a generalized template of the process laid out in this post — it's not what these specific ports ran on.

sentences.en: Claude Code can deploy agents to create and run a deterministic script to produce this map.
sentences.zh: Claude Code 可以部署代理来创建并运行确定性脚本来生成此地图。

sentences.en: The prompt in the migration kit uses a workflow to create a review-and-fix loop.
sentences.zh: 迁移工具包中的提示使用工作流程来创建审查和修复循环。

sentences.en: Note: The starter kit is a generalized template of the process laid out in this post — it's not what these specific ports ran on.
sentences.zh: 注意：入门工具包是本文中列出的流程的通用模板 - 它不是这些特定端口运行的内容。

blocks.html_en: Gap inventory and skeptic reviewers

sentences.en: Gap inventory and skeptic reviewers
sentences.zh: 差距库存和持怀疑态度的审稿人

blocks.html_en: The new language has different requirements from the old language that must be met. For Zig to Rust the difference was manual memory management (C and C++ work the same way). For example:

sentences.en: The new language has different requirements from the old language that must be met.
sentences.zh: 新语言与旧语言有不同的必须满足的要求。

sentences.en: For Zig to Rust the difference was manual memory management (C and C++ work the same way).
sentences.zh: 对于 Zig 到 Rust，区别在于手动内存管理（C 和 C++ 的工作方式相同）。

sentences.en: For example:
sentences.zh: 例如：

blocks.html_en: Zig
Rust
For Python to TypeScript the gap was interfaces and contracts. Python doesn’t require a contract declaring what shape of object it will accept or what it returns, but TypeScript does. For example:

sentences.en: For Python to TypeScript the gap was interfaces and contracts.
sentences.zh: 对于 Python 到 TypeScript 来说，差距在于接口和契约。

sentences.en: Python doesn’t require a contract declaring what shape of object it will accept or what it returns, but TypeScript does.
sentences.zh: Python 不需要契约来声明它将接受什么形状的对象或返回什么，但 TypeScript 需要。

sentences.en: For example:
sentences.zh: 例如：

blocks.html_en: Python

sentences.en: Python

blocks.html_en: TypeScript

sentences.en: TypeScript
sentences.zh: 打字稿

blocks.html_en: Both Jarred and Mike created gap inventory files capturing this implicit knowledge. Jarred inventoried these gaps up front, which is what we do here, while Mike chose to translate first and then create the gap inventory by auditing afterwards. You may need to do both.

sentences.en: Both Jarred and Mike created gap inventory files capturing this implicit knowledge.
sentences.zh: 贾里德和迈克都创建了差距清单文件来捕获这些隐性知识。

sentences.en: Jarred inventoried these gaps up front, which is what we do here, while Mike chose to translate first and then create the gap inventory by auditing afterwards.
sentences.zh: 贾里德预先盘点了这些差距，这就是我们在这里所做的，而迈克选择先翻译，然后通过审核来创建差距清单。

sentences.en: You may need to do both.
sentences.zh: 您可能需要两者都做。

blocks.html_en: Check out this sample Claude Code prompt to create a gap inventory file.

sentences.en: Check out this sample Claude Code prompt to create a gap inventory file.
sentences.zh: 查看此示例克劳德代码提示以创建间隙库存文件。

blocks.html_en: Step 2 — Stress-test the rules

sentences.en: Step 2 — Stress-test the rules
sentences.zh: 第 2 步 — 对规则进行压力测试

blocks.html_en: This step involves a mini-migration that serves as a “shakedown cruise” for the larger migration.

sentences.en: This step involves a mini-migration that serves as a “shakedown cruise” for the larger migration.
sentences.zh: 此步骤涉及小型迁移，作为大规模迁移的“安定巡航”。

blocks.html_en: In this step, Jarred used one agent to translate three files using the rulebook, one agent to translate three files “like a senior Rust engineer,” and one agent to use the diff to create new translation rules. At this stage he caught two critical issues that would have created numerous issues if fanned out across all 1,448 files.

sentences.en: In this step, Jarred used one agent to translate three files using the rulebook, one agent to translate three files “like a senior Rust engineer,” and one agent to use the diff to create new translation rules.
sentences.zh: 在此步骤中，Jarred 使用一个代理使用规则手册翻译三个文件，一个代理“像高级 Rust 工程师”一样翻译三个文件，一个代理使用 diff 创建新的翻译规则。

sentences.en: At this stage he caught two critical issues that would have created numerous issues if fanned out across all 1,448 files.
sentences.zh: 在此阶段，他发现了两个关键问题，如果将这些问题分散到所有 1,448 个文件中，将会产生许多问题。

blocks.html_en: The prompt may look something like this.

sentences.en: The prompt may look something like this.
sentences.zh: 提示可能看起来像这样。

blocks.html_en: This type of stress test only works for structure-preserving migrations, where two translations of the same file are comparable line by line.  If your rulebook is a redesign — like Mike's — the equivalent test is attacking the design document directly with adversarial reviewers, then validating it with a disposable end-to-end run.

sentences.en: This type of stress test only works for structure-preserving migrations, where two translations of the same file are comparable line by line.
sentences.zh: 这种类型的压力测试仅适用于结构保留迁移，其中同一文件的两个翻译可以逐行进行比较。

sentences.en: If your rulebook is a redesign — like Mike's — the equivalent test is attacking the design document directly with adversarial reviewers, then validating it with a disposable end-to-end run.
sentences.zh: 如果你的规则手册是重新设计的——就像迈克的一样——等效的测试是直接与敌对的审阅者一起攻击设计文档，然后通过一次性的端到端运行来验证它。

blocks.html_en: Regardless, throw out any translated files. The goal is to refine the rules, not make incremental progress.

sentences.en: Regardless, throw out any translated files.
sentences.zh: 无论如何，扔掉所有翻译的文件。

sentences.en: The goal is to refine the rules, not make incremental progress.
sentences.zh: 目标是完善规则，而不是渐进式进展。

blocks.html_en: Step 3 — Translate everything

sentences.en: Step 3 — Translate everything
sentences.zh: 第 3 步 — 翻译所有内容

blocks.html_en: For the remaining steps, you run the same multi-agent loop architecture: implement, review, and fix.

sentences.en: For the remaining steps, you run the same multi-agent loop architecture: implement, review, and fix.
sentences.zh: 对于其余步骤，您将运行相同的多代理循环架构：实施、检查和修复。

blocks.html_en: You can offload implementer work to smaller models and keep reviewers on larger ones. For example, Mike used Claude Sonnet when he fanned out 12 subagents for the main migration.

sentences.en: You can offload implementer work to smaller models and keep reviewers on larger ones.
sentences.zh: 您可以将实施者的工作转移到较小的模型上，而将审阅者留在较大的模型上。

sentences.en: For example, Mike used Claude Sonnet when he fanned out 12 subagents for the main migration.
sentences.zh: 例如，Mike 在为主迁移中扇出 12 个子代理时使用了 Claude Sonnet。

blocks.html_en: The work queue should be mechanical. A batch script decides what’s done by checking whether the translated file exists on disk, then slices the pending files into batches for the implementer agents. Because the queue is rebuilt from disk every time, the migration is resumable by construction.

sentences.en: The work queue should be mechanical.
sentences.zh: 工作队列应该是机械的。

sentences.en: A batch script decides what’s done by checking whether the translated file exists on disk, then slices the pending files into batches for the implementer agents.
sentences.zh: 批处理脚本通过检查磁盘上是否存在已翻译的文件来决定要执行的操作，然后将待处理的文件分割为实施者代理的批次。

sentences.en: Because the queue is rebuilt from disk every time, the migration is resumable by construction.
sentences.zh: 由于队列每次都是从磁盘重建的，因此迁移可以通过构造来恢复。

blocks.html_en: At this stage, agents can be overly cautious with how much work they do. The fix can be a blunt, emphatic prompt instruction with context that the compiler will catch mistakes in the next step.

sentences.en: At this stage, agents can be overly cautious with how much work they do.
sentences.zh: 在这个阶段，代理可能会对他们所做的工作量过于谨慎。

sentences.en: The fix can be a blunt, emphatic prompt instruction with context that the compiler will catch mistakes in the next step.
sentences.zh: 修复可以是一个直白、强调的提示指令，其中包含编译器将在下一步中捕获错误的上下文。

blocks.html_en: Anything the translator can’t execute confidently gets flagged with // TODO(port): <reason> to be dealt with in step 4. From here on, the to-do lists write themselves: the compiler enumerates the errors, the smoke tests find the crashes, the suite reports the failures.

sentences.en: Anything the translator can’t execute confidently gets flagged with // TODO(port): <reason> to be dealt with in step 4.
sentences.zh: 翻译器无法自信执行的任何内容都会被标记为 // TODO(port): <reason>，以便在步骤 4 中处理。

sentences.en: From here on, the to-do lists write themselves: the compiler enumerates the errors, the smoke tests find the crashes, the suite reports the failures.
sentences.zh: 从这里开始，待办事项列表会自行编写：编译器枚举错误，冒烟测试发现崩溃，套件报告故障。

blocks.html_en: Two adversarial reviewers evaluate the work of the implementers using separate contexts and disagreement between reviewers goes to a third agent. When a reviewer keeps catching the same mistake across files, the fix isn't per-file. You add one sentence to the rulebook and regenerate the affected batch. The rulebook keeps growing through this step; the code never gets hand-patched against it.

sentences.en: Two adversarial reviewers evaluate the work of the implementers using separate contexts and disagreement between reviewers goes to a third agent.
sentences.zh: 两个敌对评审员使用不同的上下文来评估实施者的工作，评审员之间的分歧将提交给第三个代理。

sentences.en: When a reviewer keeps catching the same mistake across files, the fix isn't per-file.
sentences.zh: 当审阅者不断发现文件中的相同错误时，修复不是针对每个文件的。

sentences.en: You add one sentence to the rulebook and regenerate the affected batch.
sentences.zh: 您向规则手册添加一句话并重新生成受影响的批次。

sentences.en: The rulebook keeps growing through this step; the code never gets hand-patched against it.
sentences.zh: 通过这一步，规则手册不断增长；代码永远不会针对它进行手动修补。

blocks.html_en: One important design decision to note in this step is where the compiler sits. Mike ran the TypeScript compiler inside every loop, because it checks a unit in seconds. Jarred banned the compiler from the loop entirely and deferred it to the next step, because cargo takes minutes.

sentences.en: One important design decision to note in this step is where the compiler sits.
sentences.zh: 在此步骤中需要注意的一个重要设计决策是编译器所在的位置。

sentences.en: Mike ran the TypeScript compiler inside every loop, because it checks a unit in seconds.
sentences.zh: Mike 在每个循环中运行 TypeScript 编译器，因为它会在几秒钟内检查一个单元。

sentences.en: Jarred banned the compiler from the loop entirely and deferred it to the next step, because cargo takes minutes.
sentences.zh: Jarred 完全禁止编译器进入循环，并将其推迟到下一步，因为 Cargo 需要几分钟的时间。

blocks.html_en: At this step, much of the heavy lifting has been done and the prompts start to get shorter.

sentences.en: At this step, much of the heavy lifting has been done and the prompts start to get shorter.
sentences.zh: 到这一步，大部分繁重的工作已经完成，提示开始变短。

blocks.html_en: Steps 4, 5, 6 — Compile, run, and match behavior

sentences.en: Steps 4, 5, 6 — Compile, run, and match behavior
sentences.zh: 步骤 4、5、6 — 编译、运行和匹配行为

blocks.html_en: These three steps share the same loop architecture and need progressively less human judgment, so we cover them together.

sentences.en: These three steps share the same loop architecture and need progressively less human judgment, so we cover them together.
sentences.zh: 这三个步骤共享相同的循环架构，并且需要的人工判断逐渐减少，因此我们将它们放在一起。

blocks.html_en: Step 4, for example, may often dissolve into step 3 depending on the language and size of the migration.

sentences.en: Step 4, for example, may often dissolve into step 3 depending on the language and size of the migration.
sentences.zh: 例如，根据迁移的语言和规模，步骤 4 通常可能会分解为步骤 3。

blocks.html_en: Depending on the size and difficulty of the compiler step, agents may not run this at all. Jarred executed this with an orchestrator script that invoked the compiler once across the whole workspace. “Fixer agents” then ran through the error list in parallel with adversarial review. The build runs again, rinse and repeat.

sentences.en: Depending on the size and difficulty of the compiler step, agents may not run this at all.
sentences.zh: 根据编译器步骤的大小和难度，代理可能根本不运行它。

sentences.en: Jarred executed this with an orchestrator script that invoked the compiler once across the whole workspace.
sentences.zh: Jarred 使用协调器脚本执行此操作，该脚本在整个工作区中调用编译器一次。

sentences.en: “Fixer agents” then ran through the error list in parallel with adversarial review.
sentences.zh: 然后，“修复者代理”在进行对抗性审查的同时浏览错误列表。

sentences.en: The build runs again, rinse and repeat.
sentences.zh: 构建再次运行，冲洗并重复。

blocks.html_en: Reviewing the error list is helpful to catch systemic issues that may require adjustments. For example, Jarred ran into thousands of Rust module errors that surfaced after fixing cyclic imports that Zig's lazy compilation tolerated. He fixed the loop by encoding logic to classify which dependence to delete, move, or restructure the boundary.

sentences.en: Reviewing the error list is helpful to catch systemic issues that may require adjustments.
sentences.zh: 查看错误列表有助于发现可能需要调整的系统问题。

sentences.en: For example, Jarred ran into thousands of Rust module errors that surfaced after fixing cyclic imports that Zig's lazy compilation tolerated.
sentences.zh: 例如，Jarred 遇到了数千个 Rust 模块错误，这些错误是在修复 Zig 的惰性编译所容忍的循环导入后出现的。

sentences.en: He fixed the loop by encoding logic to classify which dependence to delete, move, or restructure the boundary.
sentences.zh: 他通过编码逻辑来修复循环，以对要删除、移动或重组边界的依赖进行分类。

blocks.html_en: Step 5 also has a mechanical source of truth similar to the compiler error list: crashes from the smoke test. Again, the loop fix was to group issues into categories, in this case grouping causes by root cause that are reviewed by adversarial subagents.

sentences.en: Step 5 also has a mechanical source of truth similar to the compiler error list: crashes from the smoke test.
sentences.zh: 第 5 步还有一个类似于编译器错误列表的机械事实来源：冒烟测试导致的崩溃。

sentences.en: Again, the loop fix was to group issues into categories, in this case grouping causes by root cause that are reviewed by adversarial subagents.
sentences.zh: 同样，循环修复是将问题分组，在这种情况下，按根本原因对原因进行分组，并由对抗性子代理进行审查。

blocks.html_en: Step 6 and the end of our story is comparing the programs’ behavior across the two codebases.

sentences.en: Step 6 and the end of our story is comparing the programs’ behavior across the two codebases.
sentences.zh: 第 6 步，也就是我们故事的结尾，是比较两个代码库中的程序行为。

blocks.html_en: Our files have now been translated, compiled, and smoke tested. Now it's time to shard them and run the test suite (from the prerequisite stage) against them. Tackle failures with "fixer agents" that review the failed tests against both codebases. Adversarial reviewers check their fixes.

sentences.en: Our files have now been translated, compiled, and smoke tested.
sentences.zh: 我们的文件现已被翻译、编译和冒烟测试。

sentences.en: Now it's time to shard them and run the test suite (from the prerequisite stage) against them.
sentences.zh: 现在是时候对它们进行分片并针对它们运行测试套件（从先决条件阶段开始）。

sentences.en: Tackle failures with "fixer agents" that review the failed tests against both codebases.
sentences.zh: 使用“修复代理”来解决故障，该代理会针对两个代码库检查失败的测试。

sentences.en: Adversarial reviewers check their fixes.
sentences.zh: 敌对审阅者检查他们的修复。

blocks.html_en: The next stage in this loop is a build daemon, which is the only process allowed to rebuild the binary. Fixers write patches; the daemon batches them, rebuilds once, re-runs the affected tests, and feeds the results back. This serializes the most expensive operation instead of letting multiple agents trigger it independently.

sentences.en: The next stage in this loop is a build daemon, which is the only process allowed to rebuild the binary.
sentences.zh: 此循环的下一个阶段是构建守护进程，它是唯一允许重建二进制文件的进程。

sentences.en: Fixers write patches; the daemon batches them, rebuilds once, re-runs the affected tests, and feeds the results back.
sentences.zh: 修复者编写补丁；守护进程对它们进行批处理，重建一次，重新运行受影响的测试，然后反馈结果。

sentences.en: This serializes the most expensive operation instead of letting multiple agents trigger it independently.
sentences.zh: 这会序列化最昂贵的操作，而不是让多个代理独立触发它。

blocks.html_en: When the same failure repeats across many tests, the fix moves upstream: you amend the rule that produced the bug and regenerate only the files that rule touched.

sentences.en: When the same failure repeats across many tests, the fix moves upstream: you amend the rule that produced the bug and regenerate only the files that rule touched.
sentences.zh: 当相同的故障在许多测试中重复出现时，修复会向上游移动：您修改产生错误的规则并仅重新生成规则触及的文件。

blocks.html_en: Mike's approach matters here, because many developers won't have a built-out or ported test suite. Mike had Claude create a small script to run 7 real-world scenarios against both the new port and the original Python codebase, and diffed the results. Each failing scenario got its own fix agent, and the loop ran until all seven passed.

sentences.en: Mike's approach matters here, because many developers won't have a built-out or ported test suite.
sentences.zh: 迈克的方法在这里很重要，因为许多开发人员没有内置或移植的测试套件。

sentences.en: Mike had Claude create a small script to run 7 real-world scenarios against both the new port and the original Python codebase, and diffed the results.
sentences.zh: Mike 让 Claude 创建一个小脚本，针对新端口和原始 Python 代码库运行 7 个真实场景，并比较结果。

sentences.en: Each failing scenario got its own fix agent, and the loop ran until all seven passed.
sentences.zh: 每个失败场景都有自己的修复代理，并且循环运行直到所有七个失败场景都通过。

blocks.html_en: Then he went one step further. Claude designed its own end-to-end test suite and ran it autonomously overnight, fixing what broke and re-running four nights in a row. As a result, it caught the paper cuts no scenario list would have predicted.

sentences.en: Then he went one step further.
sentences.zh: 然后他又向前迈出了一步。

blocks.html_en: • Don’t focus on individual failures. Individual failures are the loop's job. Fixer agents burn those down. Your attention belongs on the patterns.

content_html_zh:
大型代码迁移的六个步骤

下面的过程已被概括为与多种语言和场景相关。 有关更多详细信息，您可以阅读 Jared 的博客。

先决条件

开始迁移项目之前的先决条件是要有一个强有力的判断，否则你将没有退出条件或衡量成功的标准。

法官必须能够平等地评估原始代码和目标代码。 用原始语言编写的测试套件通常依赖于目标代码中不存在的内部函数。

要构建这个法官：

  • 对现有测试进行分类。 使用 Claude 来确定哪些测试可以表示为外部调用，哪些测试依赖于无法移植的内部结构。
  • 重写以实现可移植性。 将面向外部的测试转换为可以针对原始测试和端口运行的断言。 使用对抗性代理来验证重写的测试不会削弱断言。
  • 验证法官。 针对原始代码运行它以确认它通过。 然后针对故意损坏的代码运行它以确认其失败 - 未发现损坏的法官不是法官。

Jarred 有一个用第三种语言（TypeScript）编写的大型测试套件，但大多数项目并非如此。 对于他的 Python 到 TypeScript 移植，Mike 创建了七个真实场景的奇偶校验工具，并认为任何行为更改都是需要修复的错误。

在我们进入每个阶段之前，这张图可以帮助您理解。 这主要遵循贾里德的方法，每个阶段都有审查和关卡。 Mike 使用类似的循环工作流程遵循类似的整体结构，但他端到端地运行了整个迁移，根据结果修改了规则和工作流程，然后再次运行 — 每次都丢弃输出，直到第三次运行。

第 1 步 — 创建规则手册、依赖关系图和差距清单

在此阶段，我们正在创建迁移的基础：需要重构代码而不仅仅是翻译代码的位置清单、如何翻译代码的规则手册以及用于排序迁移实施工作流的依赖关系图。

顺序很重要：规则手册必须先于缺口库存。 缺口库存是根据规则手册的默认设置不涵盖的内容来定义的，并且两者在联合审计中一起进行测试。

规则手册

规则手册的确切形式取决于您必须在开始时做出的关键架构决策。 其中最主要的是，新代码是否将遵循相同的结构，或者是否将完全重新设计。

如果是前者（Jarred），则规则手册将主要是查找表，用于翻译语言之间的类型和习语，同时指出难以翻译的组件的差距清单。 如果是后者（迈克），那么它将是一个设计文档。

贾里德通过与克劳德聊天创建了他的规则手册，为每个不明确的领域制定了政策。 他还使用了专门设计的 8 个子代理，根据自己的直觉检查 8 种不同类别的常见故障模式。

依赖关系图

您需要了解文件依赖性才能有效地分解并行迁移的工作流，以便您知道首先要迁移哪些文件以及同一批次中要包含哪些文件。 某些语言和代码库具有显式清单，使这一切变得容易，但对于遗留代码库和许多流行语言（如 C/C++ 和 Python），需要发现和映射这些依赖关系。

Claude Code 可以部署代理来创建并运行确定性脚本来生成此地图。 迁移工具包中的提示使用工作流程来创建审查和修复循环。 注意：入门工具包是本文中列出的流程的通用模板 - 它不是这些特定端口运行的内容。

差距库存和持怀疑态度的审稿人

新语言与旧语言有不同的必须满足的要求。 对于 Zig 到 Rust，区别在于手动内存管理（C 和 C++ 的工作方式相同）。 例如：

Zig

fn readConfig(allocator: std.mem.Allocator) ![]u8 {
    const buf = try allocator.alloc(u8, 1024);
    // ...fill buf...
    return buf; // caller must free this — but only the comment says so
}

// A caller that forgets 'defer allocator.free(buf)' still compiles — the leak only surfaces at runtime.

Rust

fn read_config() -> Vec<u8> { 
let buf = vec![0u8; 1024]; 
// ...fill buf... 
buf // ownership moves to the caller; memory is freed automatically 
} 
// Use it after it's moved? Free it twice? Neither compiles. 
// Forget to free it? There's no free call to forget — drop is automatic.

对于 Python 到 TypeScript 来说，差距在于接口和契约。 Python 不需要契约来声明它将接受什么形状的对象或返回什么，但 TypeScript 需要。 例如：

Python

def register(handler):
    handler.setup()
    return handler.run({"retries": 3})

# Any object with .setup() and .run() works here. Which objects actually get passed in? Read the whole codebase to find out.

打字稿

interface RunResult { ok: boolean } 

interface Handler 
{ setup(): void; 
run(opts: { retries: number }): Promise<RunResult>; 
} 

function register(handler: Handler): Promise<RunResult> { 
handler.setup(); 
return handler.run({ retries: 3 }); } 

// The contract must be written down before this compiles

贾里德和迈克都创建了差距清单文件来捕获这些隐性知识。 贾里德预先盘点了这些差距，这就是我们在这里所做的，而迈克选择先翻译，然后通过审核来创建差距清单。 您可能需要两者都做。

查看此示例克劳德代码提示以创建间隙库存文件。

第 2 步 — 对规则进行压力测试

此步骤涉及小型迁移，作为大规模迁移的“安定巡航”。

在此步骤中，Jarred 使用一个代理使用规则手册翻译三个文件，一个代理“像高级 Rust 工程师”一样翻译三个文件，一个代理使用 diff 创建新的翻译规则。 在此阶段，他发现了两个关键问题，如果将这些问题分散到所有 1,448 个文件中，将会产生许多问题。

提示可能看起来像这样。

这种类型的压力测试仅适用于结构保留迁移，其中同一文件的两个翻译可以逐行进行比较。 如果你的规则手册是重新设计的——就像迈克的一样——等效的测试是直接与敌对的审阅者一起攻击设计文档，然后通过一次性的端到端运行来验证它。

无论如何，扔掉所有翻译的文件。 目标是完善规则，而不是渐进式进展。

第 3 步 — 翻译所有内容

对于其余步骤，您将运行相同的多代理循环架构：实施、检查和修复。

您可以将实施者的工作转移到较小的模型上，而将审阅者留在较大的模型上。 例如，Mike 在为主迁移中扇出 12 个子代理时使用了 Claude Sonnet。

工作队列应该是机械的。 批处理脚本通过检查磁盘上是否存在已翻译的文件来决定要执行的操作，然后将待处理的文件分割为实施者代理的批次。 由于队列每次都是从磁盘重建的，因此迁移可以通过构造来恢复。

在这个阶段，代理可能会对他们所做的工作量过于谨慎。 修复可以是一个直白、强调的提示指令，其中包含编译器将在下一步中捕获错误的上下文。

翻译器无法自信执行的任何内容都会被标记为 // TODO(port): <reason>，以便在步骤 4 中处理。 从这里开始，待办事项列表会自行编写：编译器枚举错误，冒烟测试发现崩溃，套件报告故障。

两个敌对评审员使用不同的上下文来评估实施者的工作，评审员之间的分歧将提交给第三个代理。 当审阅者不断发现文件中的相同错误时，修复不是针对每个文件的。 您向规则手册添加一句话并重新生成受影响的批次。 通过这一步，规则手册不断增长；代码永远不会针对它进行手动修补。

在此步骤中需要注意的一个重要设计决策是编译器所在的位置。 Mike 在每个循环中运行 TypeScript 编译器，因为它会在几秒钟内检查一个单元。 Jarred 完全禁止编译器进入循环，并将其推迟到下一步，因为 Cargo 需要几分钟的时间。

到这一步，大部分繁重的工作已经完成，提示开始变短。

步骤 4、5、6 — 编译、运行和匹配行为

这三个步骤共享相同的循环架构，并且需要的人工判断逐渐减少，因此我们将它们放在一起。

例如，根据迁移的语言和规模，步骤 4 通常可能会分解为步骤 3。

根据编译器步骤的大小和难度，代理可能根本不运行它。 Jarred 使用协调器脚本执行此操作，该脚本在整个工作区中调用编译器一次。 然后，“修复者代理”在进行对抗性审查的同时浏览错误列表。 构建再次运行，冲洗并重复。

查看错误列表有助于发现可能需要调整的系统问题。 例如，Jarred 遇到了数千个 Rust 模块错误，这些错误是在修复 Zig 的惰性编译所容忍的循环导入后出现的。 他通过编码逻辑来修复循环，以对要删除、移动或重组边界的依赖进行分类。

第 5 步还有一个类似于编译器错误列表的机械事实来源：冒烟测试导致的崩溃。 同样，循环修复是将问题分组，在这种情况下，按根本原因对原因进行分组，并由对抗性子代理进行审查。

第 6 步，也就是我们故事的结尾，是比较两个代码库中的程序行为。

我们的文件现已被翻译、编译和冒烟测试。 现在是时候对它们进行分片并针对它们运行测试套件（从先决条件阶段开始）。 使用“修复代理”来解决故障，该代理会针对两个代码库检查失败的测试。 敌对审阅者检查他们的修复。

此循环的下一个阶段是构建守护进程，它是唯一允许重建二进制文件的进程。 修复者编写补丁；守护进程对它们进行批处理，重建一次，重新运行受影响的测试，然后反馈结果。 这会序列化最昂贵的操作，而不是让多个代理独立触发它。

当相同的故障在许多测试中重复出现时，修复会向上游移动：您修改产生错误的规则并仅重新生成规则触及的文件。

迈克的方法在这里很重要，因为许多开发人员没有内置或移植的测试套件。 Mike 让 Claude 创建一个小脚本，针对新端口和原始 Python 代码库运行 7 个真实场景，并比较结果。 每个失败场景都有自己的修复代理，并且循环运行直到所有七个失败场景都通过。

然后他又向前迈出了一步。 克劳德设计了自己的端到端测试套件，并连夜自主运行它，修复了故障并连续四个晚上重新运行。 结果，它遇到了任何场景列表都无法预测的剪纸问题。

教训是，缺少的测试套件不会阻止这一步。 如果你无法继承裁判，请让克劳德建造一个。 不管怎样，你的原始代码库都是事实。

代码迁移最佳实践

每一次跑步都教会了我们一些前一次没有学到的东西。 可以肯定的是，您的下一次迁移将教给您本指南无法教给您的东西。 但每个项目都坚持一些做法：

  • 不要盲目遵循本指南。 每次迁移都是不同的。 将此作为起点，并在做出承诺之前与 Claude 一起规划您的具体迁移。
  • 不要关注个人的失败。 个别失败是循环的工作。 固定剂将其烧毁。 你的注意力集中在模式上。
  • 使审查变得对抗性和验证变得机械化。 对抗性审查允许运行更长时间的任务，并且通常值得消耗代币。 让脚本——编译器、差异、测试套件——成为裁判。
  • 不要对所有事情都使用最大的模型。 代币支出集中在您的循环中，因此请谨慎设计它们。 较小的型号可以很好地处理大容量实施扇出；为审阅者和任何编写其他代理将遵循的规则的内容保存最大的模型。
  • 提前加载人力时间。 规则手册和压力测试是最耗时的。 之后的一切大部分都是队列烧毁。
  • 使工作队列机械化且可恢复。 完成应该意味着“输出文件存在于磁盘上”。

检查循环结果，而不是代码

Jarred 的 Bun 迁移现已投入生产，尽管每次迁移都需要权衡。 例如，大约 4% 的 Rust 代码位于“不安全”块内，其中大部分是 C/C++ 边界处的单行指针操作。

但新的代码库明显更好。 团队工具可以检测到的每个内存泄漏都已得到修复：2,000 次重复构建的一个基准测试内存从 6,745 MB 下降到 609 MB。 Linux 和 Windows 上的二进制文件小了 19%。 跨语言优化使其在 HTTP 服务和实际工作负载（例如 next build 和 tsc）方面速度提高了 2-5%。

考虑一下是否是时候重新计算一下长期推迟的迁移的计算结果了。 选择您一直可以忍受的代码库，并询问 Claude 其迁移过程是什么样的。
